# Questions in Livestream 227

Live Stream #227: Just One More Question

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=715" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:55</a> | How should the differences between the Masoretic Text and the Septuagint version of Esther be explained? | He thinks the Septuagint often took expansive or combined readings, so the longer Greek Esther is not simply proof of corruption. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=929" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:29</a> | Would we know more about Septuagint source texts if the Library of Alexandria had not burned? | Probably not, because the main holdings had already been dispersed long before the later destruction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=1048" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:28</a> | Did the Library of Alexandria burn multiple times? | Yes. He says it burned under Julius Caesar and later during Christian-pagan riots, but by the later burning it was already greatly reduced. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=1124" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:44</a> | How should Christians answer slander and escalating political hatred after Charlie Kirk's death? | He says to reject slander, tell the truth, pray, and refuse to become the same hateful thing one condemns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=1210" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:10</a> | Wouldn't defamation be an easy win in court? | No. He says defamation cases are expensive, risky, often have low awards, and may cost more than they recover. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=2052" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:12</a> | Is he related to Peter Falk? | He says he does not know of any relation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=2121" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:21</a> | What is science, and how should it be understood? | He defines it as a repeatable method of hypothesis, experiment, conclusion, and repetition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=2415" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:15</a> | Do Christians misunderstand the importance of tough love? | Yes, often by confusing love with license; he says true tough love aims at correction rather than abuse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=2633" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:53</a> | What about natural history? Is it science? | He treats natural history as a reconstruction discipline that grew from natural philosophy, not hard science in the strict sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=2845" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:25</a> | What are his thoughts on censorship in storytelling, especially the Texas anime controversy? | He opposes the law as overly broad, censorious, and likely protectionist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=3223" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:43</a> | Does he have thoughts on the recent finds from et-Tell? | He has not seen the papers yet and is withholding judgment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=3294" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:54</a> | How should churches deal with petty disputes, gossip, and backstabbing? | He says gossip and slander should be treated as real sins under church discipline, not as harmless behavior. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=3651" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:51</a> | Did Egyptians ever leave non-serious inscriptions such as dirty jokes or brothel graffiti? | He does not point to brothel inscriptions, but says Egypt did preserve erotic papyri and other non-serious material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=3709" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:49</a> | Did Egyptians have political cartoons? | He says no, because political cartoons could have carried a death-penalty risk in that context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=3940" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:40</a> | How close are scholars to AI that can accurately read and translate ancient inscriptions? | Still far away, because ancient scripts are too nuanced, sparse, and structurally difficult for current AI. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=4472" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:32</a> | Did he ever say the Merneptah Stele's mention of Israel is not really Israel? | No. He says Josh Bowen made that claim, while he thinks the stele does mention Israel or Israelites. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=5596" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:16</a> | What is his view of God's relation to time, and how does that affect Jeremiah 1:5? | He places God outside time and reads the passage as a statement about foreknowledge rather than God being trapped inside time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=5765" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:05</a> | If God is outside time and space, where is he not, and how should omnipresence be understood? | He rejects a literal everywhere-in-creation view as collapsing into pantheism, while still affirming God's knowledge, power, and ability to be present. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=6258" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:44:18</a> | Is God with those who suffer terribly at the hands of sin? | Yes. He says God knows, can comfort, and is present even amid horrific evil. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=7013" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:53</a> | Did Pilate need Rome's approval to confirm the crucifixion? | No. He says Pilate had the authority to make that decision as prefect. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=7292" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:32</a> | Was the way Roman governors extracted wealth from provinces considered embezzlement? | Not by Roman standards, as long as Rome received its share; provincial extraction was treated as part of a governor's reward. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=7568" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:08</a> | Why did Jesus eat with tax collectors and sinners, and why were tax collectors viewed so badly? | He says tax collectors were treated as especially bad because they collaborated with Rome and profited from the occupation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=7616" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:56</a> | What about the incident of Roman standards or flags being placed in the Jewish temple? | He says there were Roman attempts to impose imperial symbols on temple space, and they provoked outrage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=7771" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:31</a> | Does the account of Jonah coincide with the Assyrian crisis around 760 BC under Ashur-Dan III? | He says it can fit that period, which was a low point for Assyria with revolts, plagues, and contraction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=7958" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:38</a> | Would the solar eclipse of 763 BC have prepared Nineveh to receive Jonah's message? | Not necessarily; he says Assyrians did note eclipses, but whether this prepared them is hard to say. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=8008" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:28</a> | Would he look at a video responding to his view of the correct reading of Psalm 22? | No. He says he does not want to give that critic more attention. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=8111" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:11</a> | What is the issue with Psalm 22:16? | He says the dispute is whether the text should be read as "pierced" or "like a lion"; he favors "pierced" but is not dogmatic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=8547" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:27</a> | Could Ashur-Dan III staying in Assyria during four years of his reign be due to Jonah's preaching? | He doubts it and says the period is too foggy to know what was happening. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=8596" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:16</a> | What are his thoughts on virtue ethics? | He treats it as usable, so long as it is tied to real moral formation instead of empty abstraction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=8665" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:25</a> | Did the saints rise in Matthew 27, or is Licona right that they did not? | He disagrees with Licona and reads the passage as a real, limited resurrection event that points to Christ conquering death. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=9421" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:01</a> | Why don't documentary hypothesis proponents notice the cognitive load of explaining older-looking details as oral tradition or redaction? | He says theological education often overloads students so they lack time to question what they are taught. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=9628" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:28</a> | What are his thoughts on the Akkadian terms and Gilgamesh-related question raised in chat? | He says the question is not clear and Akkadian lexicography is outside his specialty, so he cannot answer it well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=9725" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:05</a> | Was 2 Maccabees 5:4 referring to a comet apparition as a revelation? | He says he is not an Apocrypha specialist and would need to look it up offline. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=10048" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:28</a> | Why do people love fantastic stories of perfect preservation instead of the messier way God works in text criticism? | He says people love myths and simple claims because they avoid the messy details of textual criticism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=10551" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:51</a> | Who is Melchizedek? | He identifies Melchizedek as the king of Salem, a Jebusite/Amorite priest-king contemporary with Abraham. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=10704" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:24</a> | Have archaeologists found Melchizedek's temple near Jerusalem? | He does not think it has been securely found, though chat notes a debated small temple from roughly the right period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=11218" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:58</a> | Is it common for a standard academic reference work to have real errors? | Yes. He says important works commonly have errors, and the question is whether those errors undermine the useful scholarship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=11507" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:11:47</a> | Has he ever heard a spirit or demon make a sound? | No. He says the only apparition he has seen was visual and silent, though others report hearing things. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=11612" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:13:32</a> | Is there an issue with viewing the ten plagues as polemics against Egyptian gods? | Yes. He says the theory breaks down when specific gods must be matched to each plague, and the text only explicitly says this of Passover. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=12064" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:04</a> | Do we have evidence that Christians celebrated on Sunday in the first centuries? | Yes. He says early Christians met both with synagogue communities on Saturday and as Christians on Sunday. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=12305" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:05</a> | What does he think about Ecclesiastes? | He likes the book, though he was not familiar with the specific "Ecclesiastes and Blues" reference mentioned in chat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=12524" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:44</a> | Why was Samson seen as a judge? | He says judges were liberators meant to rescue Israel from oppression and idolatry, even when they did the job poorly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=12767" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:47</a> | What is his favorite of the four Gospels? | John, because he is especially drawn to its heavy theology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=12829" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:49</a> | How strong is the "lame origin story" argument for Exodus historicity? | He says it is suggestive of historical grounding, but should not be overstated as proof. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=13154" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:39:14</a> | Why does Cam seem so interested in the Shroud recently? | He connects it to Catholic-convert culture and says there may also be a platform or audience incentive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=13445" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:44:05</a> | Where did he get the idea of the five stages of ideological violence? | He says he first heard it in a mid-1990s NPR-broadcast lecture, but he can no longer identify the speaker. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=13796" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:49:56</a> | In which century was the hexagram used as a symbol for the Star of David? | He places it around the late second to early first century BC in the context of ossuary dating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=13842" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:42</a> | Are the United Church and Orthodox Church basically the same? | No. He says they are nothing alike and sharply contrasts the United Church's liberalism with Orthodoxy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=14212" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:56:52</a> | Can he explain the timeline for the late versus early date of the Old Testament or Exodus chronology? | He contrasts the late and early Exodus timelines and says the "400 years" issue depends on whether the period includes both Egypt and Canaan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=14522" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:02:02</a> | What is his view of exorcism and the practice of it? | He says exorcism is a church practice that should be done carefully and prayerfully after excluding mental-health causes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=14741" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:05:41</a> | Does he know much about Ed and Lorraine Warren? | Yes, but he does not recommend their work because he considers their methodology unsound and sometimes biased. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=14972" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:09:32</a> | Why did Christian tarot cards stop being used that way? | He says tarot was taken over for divination, though a small number still use older decks for Christian teaching. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=15043" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:10:43</a> | What is the worst-case scenario with Ouija boards? | He says the worst case is demonic possession because such tools function as devices of mediumship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=15396" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:16:36</a> | Is calling for Bloody Mary in a mirror a form of divination? | Yes. He treats it as a bad spiritual practice and says not to play with it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=15559" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:19:19</a> | Do people get interested in the occult because they have too much time on their hands? | No. He says many start flippantly or skeptically, then get pulled deeper when they think there is something to it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=15612" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:20:12</a> | What are his thoughts on Catholic and Orthodox prayers to dead saints? | He says they are requests for intercession rather than worship, but he questions their effectiveness compared with praying to God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=15918" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:25:18</a> | Can people ask God to pass a message to those in Sheol? | He does not think so, saying Abraham's bosom has been relocated and is no longer in proximity to Sheol. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=15978" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:26:18</a> | Do demons hide themselves from secular people in order to deceive them? | Sometimes. He says spirits use different strategies, including hiding, frightening, or misleading people. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/am_14bsWNQQ?t=16166" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:29:26</a> | Is it possible to channel good spirits such as God's angels? | No. He says good spirits would not enter in a way that displaces the role of the Holy Spirit. |
